title: Structured Observability, Health, Profiling, Capacity, and Incidents · Language Lens: Health Live Ready Startup
summary: Logs contain tokens and raw paths, metrics use user IDs, traces mark every 404 as a server error, readiness ignores database saturation, and a p50 benchmark drives release. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsgo health live ready startup increment in low-cardinality service evidence and incident dashboard. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate liveness, readiness, startup, dependency, drain, and deep diagnostics; bound their cost; and prevent sensitive disclosure.
Apply current OpenTelemetry server span kind, low-cardinality route, method, status, error.type, network and server attributes, propagation, sampling, and 4xx semantics.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Predict Health Live Ready Startup · Theory Health Live Ready Startup
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 6437 in a bursting intake service near its concurrency ceiling. Build deterministic Go evidence to use bounded route telemetry and distinguish server errors from readiness capacity; then saturate one admission budget while another tenant remains serviceable. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
